name: Machine Learning Fundamentals
focus: Supervised learning — when would you pick it?
Model evaluation metrics: precision, recall, F1-score, AUC-ROC
avoid: Purely theoretical math proofs with no applied context
Obscure statistical tests unrelated to the candidate's resume

name: Deep Learning & Neural Networks
focus: CNN, RNN, Transformer architectures — explain when you would use each
Transfer learning and fine-tuning pre-trained models
avoid: Niche research-only architectures the candidate has not listed on their resume

name: Python Programming & Data Engineering
focus: Python best practices: generators, decorators, context managers
Data manipulation with pandas and NumPy
avoid: Language wars or comparisons with unrelated languages
Obscure CPython internals unrelated to practical work

name: Projects & Real-World Experience
focus: Walk me through a challenging project from your resume and the decisions you made
What was the biggest technical obstacle you faced, and how did you solve it?
avoid: Hypothetical projects the candidate has never worked on
Probing into confidential details of previous employers
